en.cta.desc: Infernux (熔炉) is for teams who want source access, clear architecture, and a workflow they can reshape. Start with the docs, read the code, and push the engine forward.
en.cta.title: Build something that truly belongs to you.
en.error.body: The page may have moved during a documentation rebuild. Search the current release index, return to the learning deck, or inspect the repository.
en.error.heading: That route is no longer on the flight plan.
en.error.hint: Tip: press Ctrl/⌘ + K from any main page, or / inside generated documentation, to reopen this search.
en.error.title: Signal lost · Infernux
en.footer.tagline: Open code, explicit architecture, and a render stack you can actually reason about.
en.hero.subtitle: Programmable render stack · Unity-like API shape · high-performance core
en.home.capabilities.ai.body: The FastMCP editor plane couples hierarchical discovery (mcp.catalog.*), subsystem guides with knowledge-token gates, configurable capability profiles, and trace/evolution hooks so projects can extend MCP safely—not brittle glue scripts. Scene/asset guards, transactional workflows where enabled, and structured recovery payloads (stop_repeating, suggested next tools) steer assistants toward validated edits.
en.home.capabilities.ai.title: Self-evolving MCP with strong validation
en.home.capabilities.animation.body: Sprite-based SpiritAnimator, AnimClip2D and AnimClip3D assets, embedded FBX takes, skeletal animation playback, skinned mesh rendering, and an FSM editor for state machines.
en.home.capabilities.animation.title: 2D / 3D animation stack
en.home.capabilities.asset.body: GUID-based AssetDatabase, .meta sidecar files, dependency tracking, scene serialization, asset previews, Nuitka-based standalone build, and a PySide6 Hub launcher.
en.home.capabilities.asset.title: Asset & project pipeline
en.home.capabilities.editor.body: Hierarchy, Inspector, Scene View, Game View, Project, Console, UI editor, Toolbar, gizmos, multi-selection, undo / redo, and play-mode scene isolation in a single shell.
en.home.capabilities.editor.title: 12-panel editor shell
en.home.capabilities.intro: The engine is still in active development: we are tightening every subsystem to reach a solid technical preview. Open issues and send PRs on GitHub.
en.home.capabilities.physics.body: Rigid bodies, colliders, scene queries, collision callbacks, layer filtering, and scene-synchronized transforms backed by Jolt Physics.
en.home.capabilities.physics.title: Jolt rigid bodies
en.home.capabilities.render.body: Forward and deferred rendering, PBR materials, cascaded shadows, MSAA, shader reflection, post-processing stack, and RenderGraph-based pass scheduling.
en.home.capabilities.render.title: Vulkan render core
en.home.capabilities.script.body: Unity-style component lifecycle, serialized inspector fields, decorators, input APIs, coroutines, prefabs, hot-reload, and a built-in @njit decorator that opts in to Numba JIT with automatic pure-Python fallback.
en.home.capabilities.script.title: Python gameplay layer
en.home.capabilities.title: Where the engine stands today.
en.home.demo.intro: A dual-axis sine-wave workload updates a 100 × 100 cube grid through the Batch + JIT path while one directional light and one shared material keep the render setup explicit.
en.home.demo.panel.item1: Test environment: 1920 × 1080, V-Sync off, Windows 11, Core Ultra 7 265K, RTX 5070 Ti and 64 GB DDR5-6400.
en.home.demo.panel.item2: After a 60-frame warm-up, the 10,000-cube single-material workload averaged 127 FPS in the Infernux editor and 171 FPS at runtime over 300 frames.
en.home.demo.panel.item3: Unity 6 is included only as a reference: shading complexity, batching and editor maturity differ. Read the methodology and complete tables before comparing results.
en.home.demo.panel.title: Benchmark evidence and limits
en.home.demo.title: 10,000-cube reference workload running inside the editor.
en.home.hero.description: Infernux (熔炉) is a game engine built from a Python–C++ split: C++17 powers the high-performance core; Python 3.12 drives editor tooling and is the gameplay scripting layer for developers.
en.home.hero.kicker: Open source · MIT · Win64 (broader platforms in development)
en.home.hero.platform: Windows 10/11 x64 · v0.2.9 / 0.3.0 preview · broader platforms in development
en.home.hero.title: Infernux — the forge is lit. Come stoke the fire.
en.home.manifesto: Infernux stands on a simple idea: commercial engine licenses keep getting heavier, and indies and small teams pay the price. When “vendor freedom” comes with a growing bill, open source stops being optional — it’s the answer. That is what 熔炉 is for.
en.home.status.card1.body: Scene documents, serialization, asset indexing, rendering, physics, editor interaction, runtime UI, animation, build output, and distribution were reworked around stricter native ownership while Python remains the public gameplay and tooling layer.
en.home.status.card1.title: Large architecture update
en.home.status.card2.body: The editor-side MCP Harness now drives deterministic game and engine validation, while runtime packs and compressed exports make release builds smaller and faster to prepare.
en.home.status.card2.title: Agent-assisted validation and distribution
en.home.status.intro: This preview combines a broad engine refactor with new VFX, capture, packaging, performance, and editor-side MCP workflows. It is much larger than an automation-only release.
en.home.status.title: Release 0.2.9: a large refactor and an early look at 0.3.0.
en.home.system.card1.body: We borrow interaction patterns from familiar tools and engines to reduce switching friction. There is a Figma-like standalone UI editor and a Python runtime that mirrors Unity’s at play time.
en.home.system.card1.title: Principle I · Low migration cost
en.home.system.card2.body: Heavy features like the render path exist to be extended, not worshipped. Programmable pipelines and other advanced APIs are shaped so newcomers can follow them without fighting the engine.
en.home.system.card2.title: Principle II · Intuitive first
en.home.system.card3.body: The engine is and will stay as free for indies and small teams to use commercially as we can make it, so more developers can reach state-of-the-art technology.
en.home.system.card3.title: Principle III · No business trap
en.home.system.intro: The design targets engineering control instead of platform lock-in. Each principle cuts hidden cost for teams shipping real games and real tools.
en.home.system.title: Three ideas the whole engine is built around.
en.pageTitle.index: 熔炉 · Infernux — Open game engine
en.roadmap.hero.description: Current releases, planned systems, and the work leading toward 1.0.
en.roadmap.hero.title: Infernux roadmap
en.roadmap.lanes.card1.item1: Animation state machines, Timeline, and rigged character workflows beyond the 0.2.9 preview foundation.
en.roadmap.lanes.card1.item2: Advanced UI controls (ScrollView, Slider, layout groups).
en.roadmap.lanes.card1.item3: Safer asset rename and dependency repair paths.
en.roadmap.lanes.card1.title: Authoring band
en.roadmap.lanes.card2.item2: Terrain system for larger production scenes.
en.roadmap.lanes.card2.item3: Better scene composition for reusable runtime content.
en.roadmap.lanes.card2.title: Content band
en.roadmap.lanes.card3.item2: A more robust project lifecycle from prototype to release.
en.roadmap.lanes.card3.title: Shipping band
en.roadmap.lanes.card4.item1: Keep render and tooling internals explicit as the engine grows.
en.roadmap.lanes.card4.item2: Avoid accidental complexity that would erase the engine's main advantage.
en.roadmap.lanes.card4.item3: Preserve scriptability while strengthening native ownership boundaries.
en.roadmap.lanes.card4.title: Architecture band
en.roadmap.lanes.intro: Each band answers one practical question: can teams author faster, can content scale, can projects ship, and can the architecture stay readable while the engine grows?
en.roadmap.lanes.title: Release areas
en.roadmap.priorities.card1.body: Extend deterministic, frame-stepped project control, scene and asset editing, and structured feedback so the MCP Harness can cover more real development workflows.
en.roadmap.priorities.card1.title: MCP workflow completion
en.roadmap.priorities.card2.body: Build a Niagara-style particle system with reusable emitters, modules, rendering paths, and practical debugging tools.
en.roadmap.priorities.card2.title: Niagara-style particle system
en.roadmap.priorities.card3.body: Add visual shader and particle authoring on the shared graph foundation, with consistent editing, serialization, compilation, and preview behavior.
en.roadmap.priorities.card3.title: Shader Graph & Particle Graph
en.roadmap.priorities.intro: The next work is centered on agent-assisted development and a complete visual effects authoring path.
en.roadmap.priorities.title: Next priorities
en.roadmap.release.current.item1: A large-scale refactor across memory, serialization, editor interaction, physics and runtime ownership, and the Python/C++ boundary, with broad performance work throughout the engine.
en.roadmap.release.current.item2: Introduces the MCP Harness, expands Timeline and animation workflows, improves rendering throughput, and reduces release package size.
en.roadmap.release.current.title: Performance and architecture overhaul
en.roadmap.release.long.item1: Networking foundations and a more complete project lifecycle from prototype to release.
en.roadmap.release.long.item2: Move from Windows-first technical preview toward a multi-platform production engine.
en.roadmap.release.long.title: Networking and project lifecycle
en.roadmap.release.mid.item1: GPU particle system, terrain, and broader model and material pipeline.
en.roadmap.release.mid.item2: Expand the range of visual content the engine can produce without collapsing under content complexity.
en.roadmap.release.mid.title: Particles, terrain, and content pipeline
en.roadmap.release.next.item1: Complete more of the MCP workflow for deterministic project editing, testing, and structured engine feedback.
en.roadmap.release.next.item2: Develop a Niagara-style particle system together with Shader Graph and Particle Graph authoring.
en.roadmap.release.next.title: MCP and visual effects authoring

zh.cta.desc: 熔炉面向那些希望拥有源码访问权、清晰架构与可重塑工作流的团队。先看文档，再看代码，然后把这台引擎继续向前推。
zh.cta.title: 去构建真正属于你自己的东西。
zh.error.body: 该页面可能在文档重建时移动。请搜索当前版本索引、返回学习中心，或检查代码仓库。
zh.error.heading: 这条路线已不在当前飞行计划中。
zh.error.hint: 提示：可在任意主页面按 Ctrl/⌘ + K，或在生成文档中按 /，重新打开此搜索。
zh.error.title: 信号丢失 · Infernux
zh.footer.tagline: 开放代码、明确架构，以及一套你能真正讲清楚的渲染栈。
zh.hero.subtitle: 可编程渲染管线 · 类Unity API架构 · 高性能内核
zh.home.capabilities.ai.body: 编辑器内置 FastMCP 服务：二级工具目录（mcp.catalog.*）、完整签名与示例、子系统指南 + 知识令牌门禁敏感写入；能力矩阵与追踪 / 自进化项目工具可按配置开启，让 Agent 工具链随项目成长而不是堆一次性脚本。配合场景与资产安全护栏、可选事务，以及带 stop_repeating 与下一步工具建议的结构化恢复信息，降低模型盲重试。
zh.home.capabilities.ai.title: 自进化 + 强验证的 MCP 控制面
zh.home.capabilities.animation.body: 基于精灵的 SpiritAnimator、AnimClip2D 与 AnimClip3D 资产、FBX 内嵌动作、骨骼动画播放、蒙皮网格渲染，以及用于编辑状态机的 FSM 面板。
zh.home.capabilities.animation.title: 2D / 3D 动画栈
zh.home.capabilities.asset.body: 基于 GUID 的 AssetDatabase、.meta 附属文件、依赖追踪、场景序列化、资产预览、Nuitka 独立构建，以及基于 PySide6 的 Hub 启动器。
zh.home.capabilities.asset.title: 资产与项目管线
zh.home.capabilities.editor.body: Hierarchy、Inspector、Scene View、Game View、Project、Console、UI 编辑、Toolbar、Gizmo、多选、撤销 / 重做与 Play 模式场景隔离全部集中在同一套外壳。
zh.home.capabilities.editor.title: 12 面板编辑器外壳
zh.home.capabilities.intro: 目前，引擎还处于紧张地开发阶段，我们正在一步一步完善各个功能，以期达到技术预览的标准。欢迎在Github上提Issue或是贡献自己的PR！
zh.home.capabilities.physics.body: 刚体、碰撞体、场景查询、碰撞回调、层过滤与场景同步变换，全部基于 Jolt Physics。
zh.home.capabilities.physics.title: Jolt 刚体物理
zh.home.capabilities.render.body: 前向 / 延迟渲染、PBR、级联阴影、MSAA、Shader 反射、后处理栈，以及基于 RenderGraph 的 Pass 调度。
zh.home.capabilities.render.title: Vulkan 渲染内核
zh.home.capabilities.script.body: 类 Unity 的组件生命周期、Inspector 可见的序列化字段、装饰器、输入 API、协程、预制体、热重载，以及内置 @njit 装饰器：自动接入 Numba JIT，并在不可用时降级为纯 Python。
zh.home.capabilities.script.title: Python 玩法层
zh.home.capabilities.title: 引擎现状。
zh.home.demo.intro: Batch + JIT 路径逐帧更新 100 × 100 的立方体网格，使用双轴正弦波、一个方向光和一种共享材质，让渲染条件保持明确。
zh.home.demo.panel.item1: 测试环境：1920 × 1080、关闭垂直同步、Windows 11、Core Ultra 7 265K、RTX 5070 Ti 与 64 GB DDR5-6400。
zh.home.demo.panel.item2: 预热 60 帧后，一万个立方体的单材质负载在 Infernux 编辑器中平均 127 FPS，运行时平均 171 FPS；数据取自随后 300 帧。
zh.home.demo.panel.item3: Unity 6 只作为参考：着色复杂度、批处理和编辑器成熟度并不相同。比较结果前请先阅读完整方法与数据表。
zh.home.demo.panel.title: 基准证据与限制
zh.home.demo.title: 编辑器内运行的一万个立方体参考负载。
zh.home.hero.description: 熔炉（Infernux）是一个由 Python-C++ 混合编程构造的游戏引擎。 C++17 用于构造引擎的高性能核心， Python3.12 则用于实现编辑器工具，并作为游戏逻辑脚本提供给开发者。
zh.home.hero.kicker: 开源 · MIT · WIN64（多平台正在路上！）
zh.home.hero.moreActions: 探索 Infernux 的更多方式
zh.home.hero.platform: Windows 10/11 x64 · v0.2.9 / 0.3.0 前瞻 · 更多平台正在开发
zh.home.hero.title: 熔炉初燃，来一起添把火
zh.home.manifesto: 熔炉的立场很简单——商业引擎的授权费用越来越高，独立开发者与小团队的负担日益增长。商业引擎把创造的自由标上价格，开源就不再是选项，是答案。而这就是熔炉引擎的价值。
zh.home.status.card1.body: 场景文档、序列化、资产索引、渲染、物理、编辑器交互、运行时 UI、动画、构建产物与分发路径都围绕更严格的原生所有权进行了重构，同时继续保留 Python 作为公开玩法与工具层。
zh.home.status.card1.title: 一次大规模架构更新
zh.home.status.card2.body: 编辑器侧 MCP Harness 现在可以驱动确定性的游戏与引擎验证；Runtime Pack 与压缩导出也让 Release 构建更小、更快地进入可分发状态。
zh.home.status.card2.title: Agent 辅助验证与分发
zh.home.status.intro: 这次预览把广泛的引擎重构与 VFX、捕获、打包、性能优化和编辑器侧 MCP 工作流放在了一起，并不是一次只围绕自动化的更新。
zh.home.status.title: Release 0.2.9：一次大规模重构，也是 0.3.0 的提前预览。
zh.home.system.card1.body: 我们从知名的工具与游戏引擎中学习引擎的交互模式，尽可能减少开发者的迁移成本。我们有一个类Figma的独立UI编辑器，也有和Unity运行时完全一致的Python运行时。
zh.home.system.card1.title: 原则 I · 迁移方便
zh.home.system.card2.body: 渲染路径等复杂功能是用来扩展的，不是用来供着的。我们尽可能将可编程渲染管线以及其它类似的高级API构造得符合入门开发者的直觉
zh.home.system.card2.title: 原则 II · 符合直觉
zh.home.system.card3.body: 引擎的本体将采用且尽可能永久对独立开发者或小团队免费开源可商用，我们希望让更多的开发者能用上最先进水平的技术。
zh.home.system.card3.title: 原则 III · 没有商业陷阱
zh.home.system.intro: 这台引擎围绕工程控制权而不是平台绑定来设计。每条原则都为做真实游戏与真实工具的团队减少隐藏成本。
zh.home.system.title: 这台引擎的基础设计围绕着三个最核心的原则。

zh.pageTitle.start: 熔炉 · Infernux — 开始使用
zh.roadmap.hero.description: 查看当前版本、计划中的系统，以及通往 1.0 的开发工作。
zh.roadmap.hero.title: Infernux 路线图
zh.roadmap.lanes.card1.item1: 在 0.2.9 前瞻版的动画状态机、Timeline 与绑定角色工作流基础上继续推进。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card1.item2: 高级 UI 控件（ScrollView、Slider、布局组件）。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card1.item3: 更安全的资产重命名与依赖修复路径。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card1.title: 创作带
zh.roadmap.lanes.card2.item2: 支撑更大场景的地形系统。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card2.item3: 改进场景组织方式，便于内容复用。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card2.title: 内容带
zh.roadmap.lanes.card3.item2: 更稳健的项目生命周期，从原型到发布。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card3.title: 发布带
zh.roadmap.lanes.card4.item1: 随着引擎扩张，让内部所有权边界与结构保持清晰。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card4.item2: 避免意外复杂度抹掉项目最重要的优势。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card4.item3: 在保持原生性能的同时维护 Python 脚本能力。
zh.roadmap.lanes.card4.title: 架构带
zh.roadmap.lanes.intro: 每条带都在回答一个具体问题：团队能否更快创作、内容能否扩张、项目能否发布、架构能否在引擎成长时保持可读。
zh.roadmap.lanes.title: 版本方向
zh.roadmap.priorities.card1.body: 继续完善确定性的逐帧项目控制、场景与资产编辑、结构化反馈，让 MCP Harness 覆盖更多真实开发流程。
zh.roadmap.priorities.card1.title: 完善 MCP 工作流
zh.roadmap.priorities.card2.body: 构建一套类 Niagara 的粒子系统，覆盖可复用发射器、模块、渲染路径与实用的调试工具。
zh.roadmap.priorities.card2.title: 类 Niagara 粒子系统
zh.roadmap.priorities.card3.body: 在共享图编辑基础上加入可视化 Shader 与粒子创作，并统一编辑、序列化、编译与预览行为。
zh.roadmap.priorities.card3.title: Shader Graph 与 Particle Graph
zh.roadmap.priorities.intro: 接下来的工作集中在 Agent 辅助开发能力和完整的可视化特效创作路径。
zh.roadmap.priorities.title: 近期优先事项
zh.roadmap.release.current.item1: 围绕内存、序列化、编辑器交互、物理与运行时所有权、Python/C++ 边界进行大规模重构，并在全引擎范围推进性能优化。
zh.roadmap.release.current.item2: 引入 MCP Harness，扩展 Timeline 与动画工作流，提升渲染吞吐，并进一步压缩发布包体。
zh.roadmap.release.current.title: 性能优化与架构重构
zh.roadmap.release.long.item1: 网络基础与从原型到发布的更完整项目生命周期。
zh.roadmap.release.long.item2: 从 Windows-first 技术预览继续推进到多平台生产引擎。
zh.roadmap.release.long.title: 网络与项目生命周期
zh.roadmap.release.mid.item1: GPU 粒子系统、地形支持，以及更广的模型与材质管线。
zh.roadmap.release.mid.item2: 扩大引擎能承载的内容范围，同时不让内容复杂度压垮架构。
zh.roadmap.release.mid.title: 粒子、地形与内容管线
zh.roadmap.release.next.item1: 继续完善 MCP 的确定性项目编辑、测试与结构化引擎反馈流程。
zh.roadmap.release.next.item2: 推进类 Niagara 粒子系统，以及 Shader Graph 与 Particle Graph 创作能力。
zh.roadmap.release.next.title: MCP 与可视化特效创作
